conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately, overall?
Tired. It feels like I am dragging myself through the day. I just want to get home and rest.
How long has it felt like you're dragging through the day?
A few months, I guess. It started around the time the kids went back to school. I just didn't have the energy to keep up with the pace anymore.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yeah, it's like my brain is foggy. I'll be sitting at my desk, and I'll just stare at the screen because I can't think of what to do next.
How long does that feeling of being stuck at your screen usually last?
It depends. Sometimes I get a task done, and I'm fine. Other times it just lingers, and I have to force myself to do something.
How have you been sleeping lately?
Badly. I wake up in the middle of the night, and I can't get back to sleep. I just lie there thinking about the stuff I have to do the next day.
How long have you been waking up with these thoughts about the next day?
A while now. I guess it's just the stress of work and trying to keep up with the bills.
Been doing anything you enjoy lately?
Not really. I used to play football with the lads, but I haven't been able to keep up with that either. Just haven't had the energy.
How long has it felt like you've been without that energy?
About a year, I think. I used to be the one who organized the games, but now I just can't be bothered.
When you think about the next few weeks, what do you expect day to day?
More of the same. Just trying to get through the week without falling asleep at my desk.
Does it feel like you're just going through the motions to make it to the weekend?
Yeah, it feels like that. I just want to get to Saturday and Sunday, and then sleep for 12 hours.
Do you find it harder to make decisions than before, even small ones?
Yeah, a lot of the time. I'll be in the supermarket, and I'll just stand there thinking about what to buy because I don't have the energy to decide.
How long do you usually find yourself standing there, trying to decide?
Just a few minutes. I usually end up grabbing whatever I can find so I can just get out of there.
Do you feel like you have enough energy for your usual day?
No. I feel like I'm running on empty.
How long has it felt like you're running on empty?
About six months now. I just feel drained all the time.
Are you still into the things you used to care about?
Not really. I used to love watching football, but now I just can't be bothered.
Has that feeling of not being bothered spread to other hobbies or activities you used to enjoy?
Yeah, pretty much everything. I used to like playing the guitar, but I haven't picked it up in months.
Is there anything weighing on your mind that you wish you could change?
Just the general feeling of being stuck. I wish I could wake up one morning and have the energy to do something about it.
What does that stuck feeling keep you from doing that you wish you could start?
Everything. I look at my friends who are all doing stuff, and I'm just sitting here.
How do you feel about how things have gone for you recently?
Like I've hit a dead end.
What feels like it's at a dead end right now?
My career. I feel like I'm 35 and I'm still just doing the same job.
How's your appetite been?
It's been off. I just eat because I have to, not because I'm hungry.
Have you noticed any changes in your weight because of that?
Yeah, I've put on a bit. Just can't seem to shift it.
Have you found yourself getting emotional more easily lately?
Sometimes. Just small things set me off.
Can you give me an example of one of those small things?
My wife asks me to help with the shopping, and I get annoyed. It's just little stuff, but I can't seem to handle it anymore.
Is it more 'heavy and slow' or 'drained after little effort'?
Heavy and slow. It feels like I'm moving through mud.
How long does that heavy feeling usually last when it comes on?
A few hours, usually. But it's always there, just waiting to kick in again.
Does rest actually help, or are you still flat afterward?
It helps for a bit, but then I'm back to square one.
How long does that bit of relief usually last?
A few hours, tops. Then I'm running on empty again.
Even after sleep, do you wake up already tired?
Yeah. It's like my body doesn't get the rest it needs, even when I'm sleeping.
How long has it felt like your sleep isn't refreshing you?
A few months, I guess. It just feels like I'm going through the motions, not actually living.
Does fatigue hit you in waves or stay steady all day?
It's steady. I just feel tired all the time.
How much does this constant tiredness interfere with your daily routine?
A lot. I just want to sit on the sofa and not move.
When you try to do something you used to like, what happens in the moment?
I get tired. I start doing it, and then I just stop because I don't have the energy.
What kind of things do you find yourself stopping most often?
Just simple stuff like cleaning or fixing things around the house.
Is it more 'not fun' or 'can't be bothered to start'?
Can't be bothered to start. It feels like too much work.
When you think about starting something, what makes it feel like too much work?
Just getting moving. It feels like I'm dragging myself through the mud.
What have you dropped or avoided lately that you wish you still did?
Everything. I used to play football with the lads, but I haven't been able to keep up with that either.
What's kept you from keeping up with the football?
Just lack of energy.
Is it lack of interest or lack of energy — or both?
Both. I used to love it, but I just can't keep up anymore.
What was it about it that you used to love?
Just the exercise, I guess. Now I'm too tired for that.
